claim_status: Trace and artifact package contract only; no browser run, no benchmark result, no trace captured, no memory result, no energy result, no Chrome-class claim, and no performance claim.

package_root_policy.source_tree_policy: Real benchmark-run artifacts must be written to a runner-owned temporary or external artifact root, then copied into any durable store only through an explicit retention decision. This checked file is a plan record, not a real artifact package.
package_root_policy.path_redaction: Durable manifests record relative artifact paths, sizes, hashes, run IDs, and stable registry IDs. They must not retain user home paths, real profile paths, secrets, cookies, tokens, or private page content.

trace_capture.tool: Windows Performance Recorder, Windows Performance Analyzer, or reviewed ETW-equivalent collection
trace_capture.redaction_boundary: ETW providers, stacks, command lines, file paths, and process metadata require redaction review before durable publication.
trace_capture.missing_proof: No ETW or WPR trace exists for this no-claim package contract.

trace_capture.platform: Cross-platform browser and renderer traces where supported
trace_capture.tool: Perfetto-compatible trace export or reviewed equivalent browser trace format
trace_capture.redaction_boundary: Trace events must use stable IDs, process roles, origin/site classifications, and semantic owners instead of raw page content or secrets.
trace_capture.missing_proof: No Perfetto-compatible browser trace exists for this no-claim package contract.

trace_capture.platform: Benchmark runner and browser under test
trace_capture.tool: Runner-generated tab lifecycle and process-topology log
trace_capture.redaction_boundary: Lifecycle records must preserve tab, origin, site-instance, frame, profile, process, and document-epoch identity without serializing private page state.
trace_capture.missing_proof: No concrete tab IDs, navigation order, lifecycle timestamps, process topology, or revival/state-loss records exist.

artifact_classes.path_policy: runner-owned artifact root; checked manifests may link only hashes or fixture-relative paths
artifact_classes.redaction: stable hardware, OS-control, corpus, network, browser-pin, and resource-attribution IDs only
artifact_classes.retention: retain with benchmark result bundle until claim expiry plus review window

artifact_classes.path_policy: runner-owned artifact root with optional checked no-claim fixture
artifact_classes.redaction: no real profile paths, cookies, tokens, secrets, or private content
artifact_classes.retention: retain with raw samples and traces
artifact_classes.missing_proof: Only the no-claim sample manifest exists; no browser-run manifest exists.

artifact_classes.path_policy: runner-owned artifact root outside the source tree
artifact_classes.redaction: review process metadata, command lines, paths, stacks, and provider payloads
artifact_classes.retention: retain when used for Windows performance, memory, energy, or regression interpretation
artifact_classes.missing_proof: No ETW trace file and hash exist.

artifact_classes.producer: browser trace exporter or reviewed equivalent
artifact_classes.path_policy: runner-owned artifact root outside the source tree
artifact_classes.redaction: stable process, frame, document, site, and semantic-owner IDs without private page payloads
artifact_classes.retention: retain when used for browser-internal timing or ownership interpretation
artifact_classes.missing_proof: No Perfetto-compatible trace file and hash exist.

artifact_classes.path_policy: runner-owned artifact root with relative paths in the artifact index
artifact_classes.redaction: no secrets, private profile paths, raw cookies, tokens, or user content
artifact_classes.retention: retain with every run, including failed and cancelled runs

artifact_classes.path_policy: runner-owned artifact root with failure-denominator inclusion
artifact_classes.redaction: review command lines, paths, URLs, crash payloads, and environment variables
artifact_classes.retention: retain with failed, unsupported, timed-out, cancelled, and successful runs
artifact_classes.missing_proof: No browser process stdout or stderr artifact exists.

artifact_classes.path_policy: runner-owned artifact root; generated local corpus only unless separate privacy review approves capture
artifact_classes.redaction: generated corpus pixels only; no private user page capture
artifact_classes.retention: retain representative failure and validation screenshots when needed for interpretation

artifact_classes.path_policy: runner-owned artifact root with manifest hash
artifact_classes.redaction: metrics, sample order, failures, and stable IDs only
artifact_classes.retention: retain every sample used in any summary or claim bundle

artifact_classes.producer: platform counter collector and browser instrumentation
artifact_classes.redaction: physical, resident, committed, shared, compressed, swap, GPU, and charged memory counters without heap payloads
artifact_classes.retention: retain when memory or 30-tab interpretation depends on the snapshot
retain when energy, wakeup, thermal, or battery interpretation depends on the sample
artifact_classes.missing_proof: No power, energy, thermal, or wakeup sample exists.

artifact_classes.path_policy: runner-owned artifact root and benchmark manifest denominator
artifact_classes.redaction: stable target IDs, reason codes, and bounded diagnostic text
artifact_classes.retention: retain for every unsupported, failed, crashed, cancelled, or timed-out case
artifact_classes.missing_proof: No browser-run failure denominator record exists.

artifact_classes.path_policy: runner-owned artifact root plus SHA-256 manifest
artifact_classes.redaction: relative paths, sizes, hashes, and classifications only
artifact_classes.retention: retain as the package root manifest
artifact_classes.missing_proof: Only a no-claim placeholder index exists; no run-generated artifact index exists.

artifact_classes.producer: benchmark runner and browser lifecycle instrumentation
artifact_classes.redaction: tab, profile, process, site, frame, document-epoch, state, timestamp, and reason-code fields only
artifact_classes.retention: retain with every 30-tab mixed or all-live result

required_manifest_records: artifact file kind, relative path, byte size, and SHA-256
hardware_id, os_control_id, corpus_id, network_profile_id, resource_attribution_id, and tab_scenario_set_id
browser target, browser pin, command line, profile root classification, viewport, cache state, extension state, agent mode, and security/isolation settings
sample order, timestamps, timeout, cancellation, warmup, cache reset, and cleanup records
trace start/stop status, collector version, provider set, dropped-event status, and redaction review status
failure, unsupported, crashed, discarded, serialized, revived, timed-out, and cancelled denominator records
prohibited_content: secrets, tokens, API keys, cookies, credential material, signing keys, and private certificates
real user profile contents, browsing history, bookmarks, saved passwords, extensions, sync data, cache entries, or local storage
private page content, screenshots, videos, heap dumps, or trace payloads outside generated or reviewed benchmark corpus material
absolute user home paths or real profile paths in durable public records
model prompts, model outputs, or agent observations that include private page content or credentials
unsupported_behavior: no ETW, WPR, WPA, Perfetto, or equivalent trace was captured
no raw sample file, memory snapshot, power sample, screenshot, video, or browser log exists
no runner-generated 30-tab artifact, process topology, site-isolation, revival, or state-loss evidence exists
no Chrome-class, faster, lower-memory, lower-energy, daily-driver, production, beta, stable, accessibility, security, or compatibility claim is supported
missing_proof: runner-generated trace and artifact package root with SHA-256 manifest
ETW or equivalent Windows trace and Perfetto-compatible browser trace where applicable
runner logs, browser stdout/stderr, environment manifest, and failure denominator records
raw samples, memory snapshots, CPU/GPU counters, power or energy samples, screenshots when relevant, and redaction review
30-tab lifecycle log with concrete tab IDs, navigation order, process topology, site-instance identity, lifecycle transition timestamps, revival measurements, and state-loss records
owner-reviewed retention, publication, privacy, and claim-bundle decision
